entry.title: Re: SAP Mobile Service Client shows several blank screens.
entry.content: Hello Bill, thanks for the answer, the metadata is correct since it works in 2 apks, in dev and quality. The only place is does not work is in the mobile svcs client

entry.title: Re: Invalid entry in Time profile in Production schedulin...
entry.content:
Maintained time profile to users and then its working.

SIMG - Advanced Planning - Apps for Material Requirements Planning - General Settings - Assign Time Profile to User

entry.title: SAP BTP MDK I am getting delta download for an offline odata client failed
entry.content: SAP BTP MDK I am getting delta download for an offline odata client failed. How to resolve can some one help me

entry.title: Error During Preview Application for Fiori Tools Generate Project
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I ran into an issue when working on this tutorial: 

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/fiori-tools-generate-project.html

There are no errors in Visual Studio Code, but when I try to view the site in a browser it's blank. I see the following error in the console:

Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 (Not Found)
send @ jquery-dbg.js:9325Understand this error
jquery-dbg.js:9325 


Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 (Not Found)
send @ jquery-dbg.js:9325Understand this error
jquery-dbg.js:9325 


Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 (Not Found)
send @ jquery-dbg.js:9325Understand this error
assert-dbg.js:34 Assertion failed: could not find any translatable text for key 'appTitle' in bundle 'i18n/i18n.properties'
a @ assert-dbg.js:34Understand this error
Container.js?eval:2 Uncaught (in promise) TypeError: e.indexOf is not a function
at q.createRenderer (Container.js?eval:2:1588)
at locate-reuse-libs.js:231:38
at Core-dbg.js:1357:5
at Array.forEach (<anonymous>)
at Core._executeInitListeners (Core-dbg.js:1356:14)
at Core._executeInitialization (Core-dbg.js:1369:3)
at Core.init (Core-dbg.js:1191:4)
at Core-dbg.js:485:9
at a (Core-dbg.js:179:5)
at SyncPoint.finishTask (Core-dbg.js:173:5)

Has anyone come across this before?

entry.title: How to add oAttachments2 to oPurchaseQuotations using B1 DI Server API?
entry.content:
Hello everyone:

  I need to add "n" number of attachments to my Purchase order, I'm using B1 DI Server API by creating XML files andt then send them to a DIServer Client. 

I see a field in oPurchaseQuotations "AttachmentEntry", should I write my attachments in this field? how?

These are my xml files 

For oPurchaseQuotations:

<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"utf-16\"?>
<env:Envelope xmlns:env=\"http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/\">
<env:Header>
<SessionID>idSession</SessionID>
</env:Header>
<env:Body>
<AddObject xmlns=\"http://127.0.0.1/SAP/\">
<BOM>
<BO>
<AdmInfo>
<Object>oPurchaseQuotations</Object>
</AdmInfo>
<Documents>
<row>
<CardCode>aCardcode</CardCode>
<CardName>aCardName</CardName>
<NumAtCard>aNumAtCard</NumAtCard>
<RequriedDate>aDate</RequriedDate>
<DocDueDate>aDocDuedate</DocDueDate>
<SalesPersonCode>-1</SalesPersonCode>
<AttachmentEntry>WHAT SHOULD I WRITE HERE</AttachmentEntry>
</row>
</Documents>
<Document_Lines>
<row>
<ItemCode>anItemCode</ItemCode>
<Quantity>aQuantity</Quantity>
<Price>aPrice</Price>
<FreeText>aText</FreeText>
<ShipDate>aDate</ShipDate>
<RequiredQuantity>aQuantity()</RequiredQuantity>
<RequiredDate>aDate</RequiredDate>
<UnitPrice>aPrice</UnitPrice>
</row>
</Document_Lines>
<Document_SpecialLines>
<row>
<AfterLineNumber>aNumber</AfterLineNumber>
<LineType>aLineType</LineType>
<LineText>aText</LineText>
</row>
</Document_SpecialLines>
</BO>
</BOM>
</AddObject>
</env:Body>
</env:Envelope>

 

For oAttachments2:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-16" ?>
<env:Envelope xmlns:env="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/">
<env:Header>
<SessionID>idSession</SessionID>
</env:Header>
<env:Body>
<AddObject xmlns=\"http://127.0.0.1/SAP/\">
<BOM>
<BO>
<AdmInfo>
<Object>oAttachments2</Object>
</AdmInfo>
<Attachments2 />
<Attachments2_Lines>
<row>
<SourcePath>fileSourcePath</SourcePath>
<FileName>fileName</FileName>
<FileExtension>fileExtension</FileExtension>
</row>
<row>
<SourcePath>fileSourcePath</SourcePath>
<FileName>anotherFileName</FileName>
<FileExtension>fileExtension</FileExtension>
</row>
</Attachments2_Lines>
</BO>
</BOM>
</dis:AddObject>
</env:Body>
</env:Envelope>

 

Thanks in advance for your response

entry.title: SAP Enable Now Producer freezes when trying to generate SAP companion from a group.

entry.title: Re: SAP AI Core Dockerfiles: Fixing Permission denied: '/nonexistent'
entry.content: That's a great post, which helps me to fix the issue and beside the fix also explains very well the background. Thanks a lot.

entry.title: Control the supplier and manufacturer of material at purchase
entry.content:
Dear all

In a pharmaceutial manufacturing we use Q info to like specific supplier to each material, we faced a problem where some suppliers are brokers that provide materials manufactured by other companies

I'm wondering if there is a way to link the manufacturers to the business partners to the material as a control and at the purchase where once the purchasing team select a supplier they choose which manufacturer to supply from

entry.title: Re: Where can I find the change number used in MM12 trans...
entry.content: Thanks for the suggestion, but for scheduled transactions (MM12), CDHDR does not have an entry. MM14 will show me that a scheduled change exists but does not give me the change number used.

entry.title: Re: SAP Service Cloud - Version 2 - Utilities add on - Ho...
entry.content:
Yes, It's possible to create cases linked to the premise.

 

Thanks

Pedro

entry.title: How sales order will be created if we don't have capacity.
entry.content:
Customer need a product of 1 pc and sales order has been created on todays date for 1 pc. It needs 1 day to produce 1 pc of the same product from production side.

Against the sales order, production order has been created and operation has been dispatched to work centers. For today the capacity is full. No more production can be done.

Now the same customer need the same product of 2 pc by today itself. But we don't have the resource capacity to produce it today. We have resource capacity tomorrow to produce it.

Now how the new quantity will be handled in sales order. Will system propose the tomorrows date?

entry.title: Re: Migrating from Suite to SAP S/4HANA Vis-á-vis SAP Business Workflow/Flexible Workflow
entry.content:
Hi, I have the following doubt:
In my company we want to implement purchase order approval and order request through fiori's “my inbox” application.
currently we have an approval strategy implemented in sap gui, then the question is: if we implement the my inbox application, could an approval strategy using sap gui be used together with the my inbox application of fiori?
i.e. if the purchase order has to go through 3 approvers, could 2 approvers use sap gui and 1 approver use the fiori my inbox app?
I have not found any official information from sap about this, and that is why this my doubt. At the same time it would help me to understand if I can do a gradual implementation (so that some users use my inbox and others sap gui) or if I have to do a complete implementation for the whole team.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Missing Adaptation after Deployment to Fiori Launchpad
entry.content: Thank you for you response. greatly appreciated. I have removed the Url and it's still not showing up. Please adavice

entry.title: Re: Migrating from Suite to SAP S/4HANA Vis-á-vis SAP Business Workflow/Flexible Workflow
entry.content:
Hi @MaximoSanchez 

Normally when you configure any type of workflow system by default sent it to User Inbox in (Business Workplace) Gui even though if you have Fiori activated but still, they can Approve or Reject from GUI. Now coming to your question (if the purchase order has to go through 3 approvers, could 2 approvers use sap gui and 1 approver use the fiori my inbox app?) yes, they can use it but for Fiori you need to some configuration, and you can easily find it online 🙂

Thanks,

Sanan Afridi

entry.title: Optional Surveys and Delegation will be available on LMS 1H2024?
entry.content: Optional Surveys and Delegation are relevant for our business learning strategies. We want to adopt New Learning Experience but cannot, until those functionalities are available. Will it be on 1h2024?

entry.title: Re: Issue with concatenate. No blanks at the end. RESPECTING BLANKS not working fully.
entry.content: The problem is that when you download on local PC, the trailing blanks are removed.

entry.title: Re: NT 2020.001, versão 1.50 – Evento de manifestação do...
entry.content:
Abri um ticket na SAP e fui orientado a seguir a nota 3421995 - Usando a ferramenta de gerenciamento de mudanças regulatórias para descobrir se a solução está planejada para uma mudança legal.

 

Na aplicação #https://app.main.regulatory-change-manager.cloud.sap/index.html não existe atualização para versão 1.5. No ticket da SAPse não existe nenhuma nota nesse app, seria necessário solicitar o suporte EoD (Expertise on Demand).

entry.title: Send the PR data to HANA without Budget check
entry.content:
We have a Requirement to send the PR details from Ariba to HANA. But there is no Budget check here.

Are there any detailed configuration for the above requirement?

Anyway, thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAP Mobile Service Client shows several blank screens.
entry.content: Does your branded client include additional plugins or extensions? It could be your apks include something referenced that isn't part of the generic Mobile Svcs client

Best regards.

entry.title: How do I retrieve the values of (SAPMF05A)RF05A and pass it into a local structure?
entry.content: I have a custom BTE and I want to retrieve the values NEWKO values of (SAPMF05A)RF05A. How do I do it?

entry.title: Connect to SAP ECC via ODBC - Microsoft SQL Server Management Studio (MSSMS)
entry.content:
PRE-REQUISITES: 

ODBC Driver Installation: The first step involves installing the correct ODBC driver that facilitates communication between SQL Server and SAP ECC. This driver acts as a bridge, translating SQL queries into a format understandable by SAP ECC. The choice of driver must match the versions of SQL Server and SAP ECC to ensure compatibility and functionality. 

ODBC Data Source Configuration: Once the driver is installed, configuring the ODBC Data Source Name (DSN) is crucial. This configuration involves: 

  • Specifying the SAP ECC server details, including the hostname, port, and client number. 
  • Inputting user credentials and connection parameters. 
  • Testing the DSN to verify that it successfully connects to the SAP ECC system without errors. 

          SQL Server Linked Server Setup: The next phase is configuring SQL Server to use the ODBC DSN: 

Linked Server Creation: A new linked server is created in SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS) to integrate with the ODBC DSN. This involves selecting the "Microsoft OLE DB Provider for ODBC Drivers" and linking it to the previously configured DSN. 

Security Configuration: Security settings must be established to define how SQL Server will authenticate to SAP ECC. This may involve mapping SQL Server logins to SAP credentials, ensuring secure and appropriate access. 

STEP-BY-STEP - ESTABLISH CONNECTION BETWEEN ECC AND ODBC 

1. Ensure that the computer that is going to be used for configuring ODBC is connected to the same server network segment; otherwise, the connection will not work. To check if the IP is reachable, use the `ping` command in Command Prompt. 

2. Open ODBC Administrator (64-bit or depending on the systems) and click on “add”, select SQL Server and click on finish. 

3. In the next screen enter the IP from the server that want to connect to (To know the IP #, ping in the system ipconfig in a CMD):  

4. Then click on next and enter the user and password configured or provided by your ECC administrator (on the server side)  

5. If is connected successfully then your system will appeared in the next screen. Select the desired one

6. Then click on finish  

7. Then click on Test

8. Connection Established: 

STEP-BY-STEP: ESTABLISH CONNECTION BETWEEN ECC AND SQL SERVER

1. Open Microsoft SQL Server Management Studio (MSSMS) or your preferred management tool, right click on Linked server and click on “New Linked Server” (Create an account)

2. Enter a name for the Linked Server and specify the product name. Then, choose "Microsoft OLE DB Provider for ODBC Drivers" and input the name of the previously established connection in the data source field

3. Click on Security Page and enter the user and password created previously.  

4. After Refreshing, right click on the linked server and click on “Test Connection” 

The system is Up and running: 

Finally you can create a request to any of your tables that you need as the following example:

I hope this blog has helped you! 👽

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP MDK I am getting delta download for an offlin...
entry.content:
What is the backend service that the destination is referencing?  

You can add the Mobile Network Trace feature to your application in Mobile Services and enable tracing before trying to sync.

After the sync fails go back into Mobile Services and stop the recording.  You can then download the trace and open in a text editor (like VSCode) and review to see if there is a better error include.

If that still doesn't help you will want to check the logs at the backend service level.  If you don't have access you may need to reach out to an admin there to have them look at the logs to see if there is more information about the failure.

entry.title: Re: MDK cloud web app has context.searchstring empty

entry.title: Re: sap BTP MDK deployee the changes every time i am gett...

entry.title: Orders has not generated based on Call Date
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have Strategy based Maintenance Plan with cycles of 3M,6M,1Y the previous order got generated on 24/05/2024 for the next order the Planned date is on 22/11/2024 and Call date is on 24/08/2024 but the call did not get generated and status is in Scheduled hold. Why so?

Regards,

Preethi

entry.title: Balance carryforward of actual data from SAP to SAC Planning system
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are using the standard CDS view `IFIACTPLNJEIC` to extract accounting data from SAP S/4HANA into SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) within a planning model via the "Import Connection" feature in the "New Model Type Using Account Dimension."

In SAC, we display the balance sheet and profit/loss statements based on this accounting data, and the business subsequently performs planning activities within SAC.

For balance sheet, it is essential to have balance carryforward data from one year to the next year to show the balances correctly.

Currently, our option is to manually load the ACDOCA-BTTYPE = RFBC` (balance carryforward) transaction data through Data Management -> Import Jobs in the model. However, due to the large volume of data (more than 100 million) in RFBC, I am looking for a more efficient way to obtain balance carryforward data in SAC planning models for subsequent years.

The main question is: How can we effectively load balance carryforward data from SAP to SAC?

Given that `RFBC involves a significant data volume, is there a more efficient method to carry forward transactions from one fiscal year to the next in SAC?

For example, while viewing the balance sheet how can we carry forward inventory balances from FY 2023 to FY 2024 in SAC, so that if a user only selects May 2024, he can view the cumulative balance, i.e., sum of entries posted in FY 2023 + April/May 2024. 

@N1kh1l @SarauShen @hartmut_koerner SAP Analytics Cloud SAP Analytics Cloud for planning please let me know your thoughts on this.

 

Best regards,
Gavin.

entry.title: Re: Date format validation in SAP Datasphere
entry.content: Thankyou Bheemesh. I will look into it.

entry.title: Re: Add new fields in FPR1 Tcode
entry.content:
The solution that worked for me:
I called FM popup_to_get_one_value to get the value of SSN No. I couldn't find a way to do screen enhancement in FPR1 to add a custom Input field.

entry.title: How to enable ASM 360 feature

How do I enable the 360 feature in Assisted Service Module for SAP Commerce Cloud? 

Kind Regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to enable ASM 360 feature
entry.content:
Hello there!

To enable the 360 feature in ASM you have to follow below documentations:

  • Integrating a New CMS Page with ASM Additional Information Framework
  • Integrating a New CMS Component with ASM Additional Information Framework

Kind Regards,
Wesley
Technical Support

entry.title: Re: Missing Adaptation after Deployment to Fiori Launchpad
entry.content: check if you are loading the correct tile/target mapping

entry.title: Re: Need a Report with Names of all employees who selecte...
entry.content:
Created an enhacement request for SAP to consider with below explanation:

Give a brief description of what business problem your suggestion helps to solve:

The proposed I-9 compliance reporting system addresses the critical challenge of efficiently tracking and reporting on non-citizen employees authorized to work for a limited period. This solution helps organizations across various industries maintain legal compliance, avoid penalties, and optimize workforce management by providing easy access to crucial work authorization data from I-9 forms, specifically focusing on Box #4 selections ("Non-Citizen Authorized to work until [Date]").

Do you have a suggestion for how you think that the issue can be addressed?

To address this issue, we propose developing a comprehensive I-9 compliance reporting system that:

1. Integrates with existing HR management systems (ONB 1.0 and 2.0)
2. Automatically extracts and compiles data from I-9 forms, focusing on Box #4 selections
3. Generates customizable reports with employee names and work authorization expiration dates
4. Provides automated alerts for approaching work authorization expirations
5. Offers secure, role-based access to ensure data privacy and compliance

This solution would require collaboration with HR software providers to develop necessary API integrations and create a user-friendly interface for report generation and customization.

Which roles (e.g., employees, managers, HR business partners, administrators) are impacted by the issue?

The proposed solution would impact several key roles within organizations:

1. HR Business Partners: Improved ability to manage workforce compliance and plan for potential staffing changes
2. HR Administrators: Streamlined process for generating compliance reports and managing work authorizations
3. Managers: Better visibility into team members' work authorization status for effective planning
4. Legal/Compliance Officers: Enhanced ability to ensure organizational compliance with employment laws
5. Executives: Reduced organizational risk and improved strategic workforce planning capabilities
6. IT Administrators: Responsible for implementing and maintaining the new system

Do you think that other companies would benefit from solving this issue? Please describe:

Yes, numerous companies across various industries would benefit significantly from this solution:

1. Healthcare: Ensure staffing continuity for international medical professionals
2. Technology: Maintain compliance while leveraging global talent pools
3. Agriculture: Efficiently manage seasonal workers and their documentation
4. Education: Track work authorizations for international faculty and researchers
5. Hospitality: Streamline management of a diverse, often seasonal workforce
6. Manufacturing: Ensure production continuity by proactively managing work authorizations
7. Construction: Maintain project timelines by avoiding unexpected worker shortages

By implementing this solution, companies can reduce compliance risks, improve operational efficiency, and position themselves as leaders in responsible employment practices. This would be particularly valuable for industries relying on international talent or facing seasonal workforce fluctuations.

 

SAP SuccessFactors People Analytics  #i-9

entry.title: Re: Quedada SAP en Honest Greens Castellana
entry.content: Thanks for taking the initiative!!

entry.title: User parameters cleared automatically(SU01 code)
entry.content:
Experts,

User parameters(SU01) got deleted automatically for a handful of users. They use abapCRM WebUI for business transactions. 

I found SAP Note 2020718 for this issue. However, when I tried to replicate the problem based on the steps mentioned in the note, I noticed that user parameters were not wiped off. I didn't find any other note or thread related to this behavior. 

Can you please suggest any solution for this behavior? 

Thanks

Bharth

entry.title: Re: The print button I created in the toolbar is not working properly.
entry.content: For information, you can obfuscate easily with Greenshot (at getgreenshot.org) e.g.

entry.title: Data Templates for Concur Data Delivery Service (DDS): RISK & COMPLIANCE SAC Story
entry.content:
Overview

The Data Templates for the Concur Data Delivery Service (DDS) Expense content package offers a powerful solution for visualizing and analyzing data captured in SAP Concur Expense. Seamlessly integrated with SAP Data Delivery Services and utilizing the SAP Analytics Cloud for data visualization, this solution provides administrators with comprehensive insights into organizational expenses, system administration, and user distributions. Its advanced analytics capabilities include robust auditing and control features essential for fraud detection and compliance assurance.

Built upon Analytical Models in SAP Datasphere and visualized in SAP Analytics Cloud, this content package encompasses a suite of detailed stories that deliver in-depth perspectives on expense management. These insights empower administrators to make informed decisions, optimize expense workflows, and enhance overall financial governance.

Serving as a crucial resource for risk detection, compliance monitoring, and analysis, the Risk & Compliance SAC story aids in identifying, investigating, and mitigating potential anomalies. It empowers your compliance team to proactively address issues, ensuring adherence to policies and regulations. Additionally, this page offers actionable insights and key data indicators to strengthen your organization's defenses against suspicious activities.

Potential Fraud Summary

This page provides an in-depth analysis of potential suspicious behavior within your organization. It highlights key metrics and indicators associated with suspicious expense reports, such as possible duplicate expense entries and attendee discrepancies. Serving as an overview for risk detection and analysis, this page assists in identifying, investigating, and mitigating potential suspicious activities in the Concur Expense module.

  • Potential Duplicate Expenses per Employee: This chart identifies employees with the highest expenses attributed to potential duplicate items, aiding in the detection and prevention of erroneous or fraudulent claims. 
  • Top Expense Categories with Potential Duplicates: This chart sheds light on expense categories prone to duplication. 
  • Geographic Tree map: This visualization maps potential duplicate incidents, providing a clear geographic distribution of areas where potential fraudulent activities may be prevalent. 
  • Top Payment Type for Potential Duplicate Transactions: This chart provides insight into payment types associated with duplicate entries. 

Duplicate Expense Items

As part of the Risk and Compliance Insights Dashboard, the "Duplicate Expense Items" page offers a detailed examination of potential duplicate expenses within the financial records or claims captured in SAP Concur. Through thorough analysis of expense data, this page identifies instances of potential duplicate entries, aiding in the prevention of erroneous or fraudulent claims. With a focus on enhancing accuracy and compliance, this page supports proactive measures to mitigate risks associated with duplicate expenses. 

  • Total Count of Potential Duplicate Expense Items Over Time: This chart monitors the historical count of duplicate expense items over time, providing insights into trends and changes in potential duplicate entries. 
  • Top Duplicate Expense Items by Expense Type: This chart identifies leading duplicate expense items by expense type, highlighting categories most prone to duplication. 
  • Duplicate Expense Items by Payment Type: This chart analyzes duplicate expense items by payment type, offering insights into which payment methods are most associated with duplicate entries. 
  • Highest Count of Duplicate Expense Items: This chart identifies the top employees with the highest counts of duplicate expense items, highlighting individuals who may require closer monitoring. 
  • Highest Amount of Duplicate Expense Items: This chart highlights instances with the highest amounts of duplicate expense items, identifying significant cases that may require further investigation. 
  • Potential Duplicate Expense - Details: This table provides detailed transactional data on potential duplicate expenses, offering granular insights into each suspect entry for further analysis and verification. 

Duplicate Attendees

The "Duplicate Attendees" page provides a thorough analysis of potential duplicate attendees across various company events and submitted expense reports. By carefully examining the declared attendee data within an expense entry line, this page identifies instances of expense entries that potentially include duplicate attendees, aiding in the detection and prevention of fraudulent or inaccurate claims. With a commitment to enhancing transparency and compliance, this page empowers organizations to take proactive measures to mitigate risks associated with potential duplicate attendees. 

  • Identifying Leading Duplicate Attendees by Attendee Types: This chart tracks the tendency of duplicate attendees over time, highlighting the most common attendee types prone to duplication. 
  • Top Duplicate Attendees by Expense Type: This chart pinpoints duplicate attendees based on expense types, identifying categories where attendee duplications are most frequent. 
  • Top Duplicate Attendees by Attendee Type: This chart identifies leading duplicate attendees categorized by attendee types, highlighting individuals or groups most frequently associated with duplicate entries in expense reports. 
  • Top Spending Employees with Potential Duplicate Attendees: This chart uncovers employees with the highest occurrences of potential duplicate attendees, identifying individuals whose expense reports may require closer examination for accuracy and compliance. 
  • Claims with Duplicate Attendees per Approval Status: This chart evaluates claims with duplicate attendees based on their approval status, providing insights into how duplicate attendee issues vary across different stages of the approval process. 
  • Expense Reports with Potential Duplicate Attendees - Details: This table presents transactional data detailing expense reports suspected of containing duplicate attendees, providing a comprehensive breakdown for thorough analysis and verification. 

Credit Card Late Fees

As a part of the Risk and Compliance Insights Dashboard, the "Credit Card Late Fees" page offers a detailed overview of late payment penalties associated with credit card transactions within your organization. By analyzing transaction data, this page identifies instances of late payments and associated fees that were declared and/or reimbursed as another expense type, aiding in the detection and prevention of unnecessary reimbursement or financial losses. With a focus on optimizing payment processes and enhancing compliance, this page provides valuable insights to help organizations mitigate potential risks related to credit card late fees. 

  • Top Employees with Approved Credit Card Late Fees: This chart identifies employees with the highest amounts of approved credit card late fees, highlighting individuals whose expenses include significant late payment penalties. 
  • Top Expense Types for Credit Card Late Fees: This chart identifies the most frequently used expense types for declaring credit card late fees, highlighting common categories where late fees are incurred and reported. 
  • Credit Card Late Fees - Expense Details: This table provides detailed transactional data on credit card late fees incurred and how they were declared by the requesting employee, offering a breakdown of expenses related to late payments for thorough analysis and management. 

 SAP Concur SAP Integration Suite SAP Datasphere SAP Analytics Cloud 

Resources:

  • https://www.concur.com/data-delivery-service
  • https://api.sap.com/package/SAPConcurExpenseDDSIntegrationwithSAPDatasphere/overview
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-sap/data-templates-for-dds-expense-administra...
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-sap/data-templates-for-concur-data-delivery-e...
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-sap/data-templates-for-concur-data-delivery-service-dds-policy-compliance-sac/ba-p/13811486
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/spend-management-blogs-by-sap/data-templates-for-concur-data-delivery-service-dds-system-management-sac/ba-p/13861522

entry.title: Re: The print button I created in the toolbar is not working properly.
entry.content: You display the ALV in CL_GUI_CONTAINER=>SCREEN0 (or equivalent), but you should avoid this in productive programs. Instead, create a Dynpro screen with a Custom control inside (to be instantiated with CL_GUI_CUSTOM_CONTAINER), and you display the ALV grid inside this container.

entry.title: Re: SAP Standard Fiori Apps : PR Approval Process
entry.content: hi @Saurabh_rode  Were you able to fix this?

entry.title: Re: How to pass dynamic field value in annotation @Consum...
entry.content:
I have a similar requirement that I would like to outline.

I would like to dynamically pre-assign the Plant filter with a value that comes from a CDS view. For example, the value of the CDS view returns exactly the one value '1010' and this value should now be output as the default value in this filter field.

My OData V2 Service Binding provides the following entities

I_Plant = Input help
Main = Main entity
PlantDefVal = CDS View, which provides exactly one value for dynamic preallocation of the filter field.

How can this be implemented in a Fiori Elements List Page?

entry.title: Re: NSE Implementation Experience
entry.content:
HI Sri, 

Is it possible to reverse the NSE recommendation once implemented ?

entry.title: Re: Behavior validation messages are not displayed
entry.content: Can you debug and check following line? %element-ProductName= if_abap_behv=>mk-on ) TO reported-product.

entry.title: SAP Developer News September 12th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/09/12/sap-developer-news-september-12th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

SAP CodeJam – A look back at recent events in India

  • CodeJam Group: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/gh-p/code-jam
  • CodeJam Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/eb-p/codejam-events
  • CodeJam topic list: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-topics/ba-p/221407

SAP Devtoberfest and SAP TechEd Watch Party

  • Devtoberfest Group Page: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • SAP TechEd Virtual:  https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html

SAP TechEd Three Reasons to Attend

  • SAP TechEd: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html
  • SAP TechEd Session Catalog: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog

Devtoberfest – One Week to Go!

  • Devtoberfest 2024 Contest https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2024-contest/ba-p/13781593
  • Devtoberfest Group https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 SAP CodeJam
1:07 SAP Devtoberfest and SAP TechEd Watch Party
2:36 SAP TechEd – 3 Reasons
3:38 Devtoberfest

Transcript

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for September 12, 2024.

[Josh] Hey everybody, I know that you're excited for SAP's Devtoberfest and TechEd. These two events are happening. Devtoberfest starts in two weeks and then TechEd is going to be October 8th and 9th. Now what's cool about this is while TechEd is being filmed by all the developer advocates and SAP experts from Germany, we're beaming it out live over the internet and you can watch it. How about with Devtoberfest sessions, you know when they're going to be happening, you and your friends can get together and you can actually have a watch party. You can do this for Devtoberfest, and you can do it for TechEd. So I offer up, if you happen to be near where I am, like you guys know I love being on stage, well I'm on a stage right now. And then out here, we've got a lovely amount of seating. We could have a watch party here in my local hometown. Just kidding. It would be probably hard to fill all of those seats because you're all working in different locations. You're all in your own offices, or maybe you're going to get together in a local restaurant. But hey, if you do want to get together, tell us about it. Give us a note, let us know that you're meeting and having a watch party and we'll promote it online and the developer advocates might even be able to talk about it or connect with you live from TechEd. So hey, if you're having a watch party, if you're watching any of the Devtoberfest sessions or if you're going to be watching any of the SAP TechEd sessions, let us know that you're having a watch party and just like my friend Luis who's down in Brazil, he told me he's trying to really get a lot of people energized to attend a lot of the Devtoberfest sessions and participate. Let us know that you're getting together and doing that if you're in a group and we'll give you a shout out. See you later, bye.

[Tom] The excitement is building as we approach this year's TechEd virtual. Let me give you a few of my top reasons to attend our live two-day community building deep dive learning program. Number one, 90 live sessions. That's right, we're talking live code, demos, lectures, and deep dive jumpstart sessions. Number two, live chat and QA. Have a question for one of our session presenters? Want to connect with an attendee with similar interest? You could do both within the chat functionality built right into the SAP TechEd platform. Number three, deconstructing the announcements. The SAP Developer Advocates will be your guides throughout the entire two-day show. We'll be there to break down the keynote announcements and deconstruct the latest innovations driving the digital transformation. Sign up now and explore your opportunities to build your expertise, sharpen your skills, and broaden your community. See you online in October.

[Rich] Hey folks, Rich Heilman here. I hope everyone had a safe and happy summer, and that you have all made it back from vacation in one piece. Because today I want to remind you that it's time to get back to work. And to help you all settle in a bit and get comfortable, yes that's right folks, it's time for Devtoberfest, and it all starts in less than two weeks from now. Everything that we've all loved from previous years is back. A ton of sessions on the latest technologies coming out of our labs. We have tutorials which you can do to earn even more points and of course our game board which shows the progress towards Nirvana. We have sessions across all of our core technology topics like ABAP and CAP, Front End, Integration, learning, AI and data and finally tooling. All of which will stream on the SAP developers YouTube channel. So there is a ton of learning opportunities for you at Devtoberfest and let's not forget the grand prize. The grand prize winners will receive a nice trip to next year's SAP TechEd event. So if you haven't registered for Devtoberfest yet do it right now. No seriously stop watching the news and go register but before you do DJ has one more call to action for you over to you DJ.

[DJ] Yeah thanks Rich and we want to hear from you. What are you most looking forward to with  Devtoberfest? Is it the points? Is it the potential prizes? Is it moving up that game board? Or is it, as it is with me, the prospect of learning cool new stuff? We want to know, so let us know in the comments and see you at Devtoberfest!

entry.title: Customer ageing on balance confirmation
entry.content:
Dear All,

We're encountering issues with incorrect ageing on our Balance Confirmation Run statements, as shown in the attached samples. Specifically, when a month has 31 days, transactions dated the 1st are being incorrectly aged as 30 days instead of being reflected in the current period. Although the system uses a 30-day cycle for ageing, the Age Analysis report ages the transactions correctly. We're seeking a solution to align the ageing on the Balance Confirmation Run with the actual period, rather than the 30-day cycle.

Thank you.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Customer ageing on balance confirmation
entry.content:
Dear All,

Currently, the system uses the following logic for the Customer Statements:

The logic takes the document date as the reference date and subtracts it from the key date.
(This is the date which is shown in column Date in the PDF File Customer Statement.)
If the difference is >= 0 and <= 29, it adds the amount to the value under the column Current.
If the difference is >=30 and <= 59, it adds the amount to the value under the column 30 Day.
If the difference is >=60 and <= 89, it adds the amount to the value under the column 60 Day.
If the difference is >=90 and <= 119, it adds the amount to the value under the column 90 Day.
If the difference is >=120, it adds the amount to the value under the column 120 Day.

Refer to the KBA 2044437 - Customer Statement And Report Aging List For Receivables Show Different Results For The Same Customer Invoice https://me.sap.com/notes/2044437 for additional details, please.

If you would like to request a system improvement, consider to create an Idea in our Influence Channel (https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/886 ), please.


Requests need 20 votes of different customers/partners to qualify for review. Reviews will happen on a regular basis twice a year, with the results being communicated in the corresponding review blog post. The ideas can be voted by other customers and partners and the most voted ones might be collected to the product backlog for future versions.

Thank you.

Regards,
Anderson
SAP Support

entry.title: SAP Inventory report
entry.content: Hi, I would like to know if SAP can have a report showing the Stock Qty, Total Receipts and Open PO quantities per part number

entry.title: Test
entry.content: This is a test

entry.title: Re: The print button I created in the toolbar is not work...
entry.content:
thank you @Sandra_Rossi 
When I create a container to be instantiated with CL_GUI_CUSTOM_CONTAINER, the problem is solved, but this time I used ALV CL_GUI_CONTAINER=>SCREEN0 because I was getting errors in other places.
Are you saying that I shouldn't use ALV here with CL_GUI_CONTAINER=>SCREEN0?

Doesn't CL_GUI_CONTAINER=>SCREEN0 also work as a container that can be instantiated with CL_GUI_CUSTOM_CONTAINER?

What is the difference between CL_GUI_CONTAINER=>SCREEN0 and CL_GUI_CUSTOM_CONTAINER? container?


Thanks 🙂

entry.title: Re: Optional Surveys and Delegation will be available on...
entry.content:
Hi Elizabeth, there was a really good webinar all about the New Learning Experience earlier today called "ILX Success: Rollout Strategies to Ensure a Successful Learning Launch". It was recorded so hopefully a link will be posted to the community at some point as it was really informative. 

Delegation will be supported from the 2H 2024 release, and I'm 99% sure they said Optional Surveys will be visible on the homepage with the 1H 2025 release - I didn't note anything down about surveys but I'm pretty sure it's the release after next, rather than the upcoming one. Certainly no later than 1H 2025 anyway.

entry.title: How to use collective Orders without goods movement?
entry.content:
I would like to know, how to use collective Orders without goods movement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to use collective Orders without goods movement?
entry.content:
Hi,

Please take a look at the following KBA:

  • 3517460 - It's not possible to use collective orders without goods movements

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: S4 Public Cloud: CDS view for batch characteristics v...
entry.content:
I found the problem. 

I_ClfnObjectCharcValForKeyDate works fine, I just didnt add the right field in the element section of the CDS view. Now I am able to see the characteristic values. 

Closing the thread.

entry.title: GROW - Scheduling Agreement Output to Ariba Business Commerce
entry.content:
We have a GROW Implementation in progress and client wants to use Purchasing Scheduling Agreements and output them to the Ariba Business Commerce Network (formerly Ariba Network). Is there anyone that has been able to successfully do this?  If so, how?  Thanks!

Cathy

entry.title: How to remove Output Items
entry.content:
We have output items created for a Sales Order, but they have the wrong configuration so we want to delete them. How can we do that?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to remove Output Items
entry.content:
You can delete an output item going through the following steps:

  1. Open app 'Change Sales Orders' (VA02) and open the desired Sales Order;
  2. Go to Menu > Extras > Output > Header > Edit;
  3. Select the output item you want to remove;
  4. Click on the button 'Delete Output'

entry.title: Re: Data Analytics Internship
entry.content:
Dear Internship Coordinator,

Thanks to David Chaviano who pointed me to your post, Data Analytics Internship opportunity, and despite the application deadline closure, I would be enthusiastic about being considered for this role in the future. I believe my unique combination of skills, experience, and eagerness to learn makes me an excellent fit for your team.

While I am early in my SQL learning journey, I am confident that real-world application in your dynamic environment would significantly accelerate my progress. I am also actively working toward my first SAP certification and am deeply interested in data analytics within the SAP ecosystem. I am currently switch career paths after being laid off October 2023 and I’ve discovered that my superpower is processing data—it brings clarity and focus to my mind, providing a sense of calm and purpose.

My professional background includes extensive experience in credentialing and auditing within the healthcare industry, leadership in maritime operations from the U.S. Coast Guard, and significant expertise in customer service and business administration. I have extensive background in regulatory compliance, and a natural ability to train anyone, which also makes me unusually adept at learning from anyone and everyone. I’m known for my problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and strong communication abilities, all of which I believe would be valuable assets to your team.

I thrive in fast-paced environments and am confident I could hit the ground running while learning simultaneously. I have no doubt my experience would allow me to contribute significantly to your data analytics efforts. I would welcome the opportunity to bring my passion for learning and innovation to your organization.

I look forward to the possibility of contributing to your team.

Sincerely,
Ryan Angel

www.linkedin.com/in/ryan-angel-b2374197

entry.title: SAC Application designer story blended models/lined dimensions

The data is displayed fine but any clicks on the table chart throw's this error.  Its a simple table chart, dimension and measure columns are from different models. Any ideas? Thank you!!

"[Application Error]:Cannot read properties of null (reading 'getDatasetId')
Correlation ID: 21192195-9278-4171-9946-442125091323 "

entry.title: Re: Document has payment method for incoming payments, no dun.notice
entry.content: thanks!

entry.title: Re: CloudEvents at SAP 🌁
entry.content:
So how do we go about extending these standard events? In the documentation for SAP Enterprise Event Enablement, there is an example in which they use an "extension attribute" on the standard Business Partner event to only send the ones for which "xsapcountry" equals "de":

Can this be done using RAP for instance?

entry.title: Re: Update Storage Location with WS_DELIVERY_UPDATE_2
entry.content: Thank you, it worked.

entry.title: Groovy Script to Save hybris/logs folder as a SAP Commerce Cloud Media
entry.content:
The Script

One possible solution for that is to save those files as a Media, it can be done by zipping the folder and using Media and Model Services to do so, the following Groovy Script does that, then you can download it from e.g. Backoffice UI.

Remember to run the script in "COMMIT" mode.

 

 

import de.hybris.platform.core.Registry
import de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.model.ModelService
import de.hybris.platform.servicelayer.media.MediaService
import de.hybris.platform.core.model.media.MediaModel
import org.apache.commons.compress.archivers.tar.TarArchiveEntry
import org.apache.commons.compress.archivers.tar.TarArchiveOutputStream
import java.util.zip.GZIPOutputStream
import java.nio.file.Files
import java.io.File
import java.io.FileInputStream
import java.io.FileOutputStream

def folderToTar = "/opt/hybris/log"
def mediaCode = "myTarFile" //Change for the name you want, media will be created on default catalog/online

def createTarGz(File folder, File outputFile) {
    FileOutputStream fos = new FileOutputStream(outputFile)
    GZIPOutputStream gos = new GZIPOutputStream(fos)
    TarArchiveOutputStream taos = new TarArchiveOutputStream(gos)
    taos.setLongFileMode(TarArchiveOutputStream.LONGFILE_GNU)
    
    folder.eachFileRecurse { file ->
        def entryName = folder.toURI().relativize(file.toURI()).getPath()
        TarArchiveEntry entry = new TarArchiveEntry(file, entryName)
        taos.putArchiveEntry(entry)
        if (file.isFile()) {
            Files.copy(file.toPath(), taos)
        }
        taos.closeArchiveEntry()
    }
    
    taos.close()
}

// TAR the folder and save as media using ModelService
def saveTarAsMediaWithModelService(String folderToTar, String mediaCode) {
    File folder = new File(folderToTar)
    if (!folder.exists() || !folder.isDirectory()) {
        throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid folder path provided.")
    }
    File tarFile = File.createTempFile("output", ".tar.gz")
    createTarGz(folder, tarFile)
    def applicationContext = Registry.getApplicationContext()
    ModelService modelService = applicationContext.getBean("modelService")
    MediaService mediaService = applicationContext.getBean("mediaService")
    MediaModel media = modelService.create(MediaModel.class)
    media.setCode(mediaCode)
    media.setRealFileName(tarFile.getName())
    media.setMime("application/gzip")
    modelService.save(media)
    FileInputStream fis = new FileInputStream(tarFile)
    mediaService.setStreamForMedia(media, fis, "application/gzip", tarFile.getName())
    fis.close()
    modelService.save(media)
    tarFile.delete()

    return "Media saved successfully with media code $mediaCode"
}

try {
    def result = saveTarAsMediaWithModelService(folderToTar, mediaCode)
    result
} catch (Exception e) {
    e.printStackTrace()
    return "Error: ${e.message}"
}

 

 

It will save the media with the name you set on "default catalog" into the "online" version in the "root" folder,
you can find it on Backoffice, into Multimedia -> Media, by search for its name.
You can use either the download button or the URL. (The URL usually works better, since this folder usually is secured)

Like this:

 

Known Limitations and Comments

The first limitation is that you will need HAC (it's a groovy script, right?), so the service you need the logs needs to have HAC available.

Also, if your service has more than one replica, you have to check if its the one replica (pod) you want, you can do that checking the ROUTE cookie on Browser and even changing it for the replica you want if needed.

Important to mind that it will consume storage, so it's always good to double check and delete it from Medias after you download it.

entry.title: Net due date change from bill of lading date
entry.content:
Hello,

I have the following situation and I hope someone could support,

Payment terms are 30 days from bill of lading date, however the bill of lading is usually issued 2 weeks later than the billing date of the invoice.
Its possible to automate that the net due date of an invoice is automatically updated once the bill of lading is populated in SAP?

With some dates for further explanation,
An invoice is issued today (billing date 12th of Sept 24) under the terms, 30 days from Bill of Lading.
The PTs customizing currently consider,
Doc due date = 12th of Sept 24
Net due date = 12th of Oct 24

However, bill of lading from the previous shipment is issued the 23th of Sept 24 (11 days after the doc. date). Once the bill of lading date is updated on SAP, there is no option that the net due of the invoice would consider the 23th of Sept as starting point to count the 30 days?

Thank you in advance.

SD Billing FIN (Finance) SAP Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable FIN Accounts Receivable and Payable

entry.title: Re: What Are You Looking Forward to Learning or Doing at Devtoberfest This Year?
entry.content: Looking forward to learn more about BTP Integration Suite... specially new EMIS

entry.title: Re: SAP Product Inspiration Series for Industries & Intelligent CX (Wave #2)
entry.content: Je suis en train de mettre en place ma structure organisationnelle dans Employee Central et je suis confronté à un défi. J'ai deux directeurs généraux qui occupent trois postes simultanément dans différentes entreprises et sont rémunérés par ces trois entreprises. De plus, certains employés sont affectés à l'administration d'une entreprise uniquement à des fins de paie, même s'ils travaillent au siège social. Il est important de noter que notre siège social n'est pas une entité juridique. Par conséquent, j'ai du mal à créer la structure organisationnelle de notre siège social.

entry.title: Re: ADT ABAP Debugger - What to do if your program does not stop at breakpoints
entry.content: Thanks all this information.

entry.title: Re: Talent intelligence hub - 2024 updates and behind the scenes!
entry.content:
Hello all,

I've submitted an enhancement request for Performance forms to display skill-specific proficiency level descriptors from the Talent Intelligence Hub. Having this information displayed on a Skills or Role Readiness Assessment PM form is critical for assessors to adequately determine skill level. If you agree that this is a good idea, I kindly request that you please vote for this. 😊

Thank you!

Kristy

entry.title: User experience and app dev sessions at SAP TechEd 2024 virtual
entry.content:
For 2024, SAP TechEd is virtual once again, with lots of great sessions grouped into six content tracks. If you prefer in-person learning, SAP TechEd on Tour consists of companion events around the world in collaboration with regional user groups (ASUG, SAUG, UKISUG, and DSAG). In this post, I’ll highlight the sessions on user experience and application development, since that’s my main focus as part of the SAP UX Engineering team.

In my many conversations with customers this year, the questions that I hear most are:

  • What is the best / fastest / cheapest path to SAP Fiori?
  • How do I move to SAP S/4HANA with all my existing customizations? I understand the concept of “clean core”, but how do I achieve it?
  • To what extent can I customize my public cloud system?
  • How much AI can I use with my current SAP setup?
  • How are you making it easier to build applications?

Here are the SAP TechEd sessions that cover these topics. Since they are all presented live from our studio in the Heidelberg AppHaus, some might not be during regular business hours in your region. Please check the session catalog to see when they are running in your time zone. If it’s at an inconvenient time, you can watch the recording.

What is the best / fastest / cheapest path to SAP Fiori?

DT104 | Enabling new, AI-driven interactions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Fiori

Enabled by SAP Fiori elements, SAP S/4HANA Cloud includes enhanced built-in AI for improved productivity, quick insights, and collaboration. See the latest user interface of SAP S/4HANA Cloud and how you can take full advantage of SAP Fiori elements through a personalized “My Home” experience, insight cards created by the Joule copilot, collaboration through Microsoft Teams and SAP Collaboration Manager, natural language for filtering SAP Fiori apps list reports, and AI-driven summaries.

Katja Zoch, VP UX Engineering, SAP

Sebastian Steinhauer, VP UX Engineering, SAP

Lecture (25 min)

How do I move to SAP S/4HANA with all my existing customizations? I understand the concept of “clean core”, but how do I achieve it?

DT100 | Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future

Explore the future of cloud ERP, focusing on the latest innovations and strategies driving digital transformation. Delve into the technological backbone of SAP's vision for cloud ERP, covering in-app extensibility, integration with SAP Business Technology Platform, AI-powered automation, and the "suite-first" approach. Learn the benefits of a clean core and its role in enabling composability, empowering businesses to adapt and evolve, and get insights into this year’s cloud ERP track sessions.

Jan Gilg, President Cloud ERP, SAP

Carine Tchoutouo Djomo, Product Manager, SAP

Track Overview (45 min)

DT101 | Choosing the right path for your business to SAP S/4HANA Cloud

SAP offers different options and guidance to companies transitioning their businesses to cloud that vary from a standardized and controlled option through SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, a flexible option based on their specifications through SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or a combined option through two-tier scenarios. Find out about the important factors, explore the right option for your organization, and get insights into SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408.

Khaled MUSILHY, VP, Cloud ERP Product Management, Head of SaaS Foundation, SAP

Lecture (25 min)

DT200 | Clean core extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Learn how to manage your custom, on-stack extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition with the ABAP Cloud development model following clean core principles. Find out about the new guidelines for clean core ABAP development in a well-known, three-tier extensibility model and how to handle transformation options for existing custom code to ABAP Cloud. Get an outlook of upcoming generative AI support and hear our plans for ABAP test cockpit to support governance of your clean core developments.

Olga Dolinskaja, Product Manager, SAP

Thomas Fiedler, Chief Product Expert, SAP

Lecture (25 min)

AD180 | Build extensions with ABAP Cloud and Joule's ABAP developer capabilities

Learn how to develop transactional SAP Fiori apps in ABAP Cloud using ABAP RESTful application programming model supported by generative AI. Explore features such as business events for loosely coupled integration scenarios and read-only "treeviews" for displaying hierarchical data. ABAP Cloud is our comprehensive development model for building clean-core-compliant apps, services, and extensions on SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP S/4HANA, both in the cloud and on premise.

Carine Tchoutouo Djomo, Product Manager, SAP

Jump-Start (25 min)

To what extent can I customize my public cloud system?

DT201 | Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Learn about key user and development tools in the ABAP Cloud development model and how customers and partners can manage their on-stack extensions to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition. Learn about the extensibility options, discover insights into integration of SAP Build solutions, and get an outlook on AI support for key users and developers.

Thomas Schneider, Chief Development Architect, SAP

Alda Dollani, Development Architect, SAP

Lecture (25 min)

DT202 | Understand how composable architecture can unleash your business value

The composable enterprise architecture can revolutionize the way businesses of the future operate. In a world where agility, efficiency, and innovation are paramount, the composable enterprise offers the flexibility to pivot and respond to business needs in real time. Learn how our innovative approach of the composable enterprise in the GROW with SAP and RISE with SAP solutions helps unlock greater business value.

Stefan Batzdorf, CTO Cloud ERP, SAP

Lecture (25 min)

AD102 | Unlock SAP S/4HANA Cloud extensibility with SAP Build

Explore the latest capabilities in SAP Build solutions to extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud while keeping your core clean. The extensibility wizard in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides direct access to various extension options both on stack and side by side on SAP Business Technology Platform. New remote function call connectivity in SAP Build enables clean core extension of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition using tier-two BAPIs.

Florian Buech, Product Management, SAP Build, SAP

Karthik Thirunavukkarasan, Principal Product Manager, BTP ADAI, SAP

Lecture (25 min)

How much AI can I use with my current SAP setup?

DT104 | Enabling new, AI-driven interactions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Fiori

Enabled by SAP Fiori elements, SAP S/4HANA Cloud includes enhanced built-in AI for improved productivity, quick insights, and collaboration. See the latest user interface of SAP S/4HANA Cloud and how you can take full advantage of SAP Fiori elements through a personalized “My Home” experience, insight cards created by the Joule copilot, collaboration through Microsoft Teams and SAP Collaboration Manager, natural language for filtering SAP Fiori apps list reports, and AI-driven summaries.

Katja Zoch, VP UX Engineering, SAP

Sebastian Steinhauer, VP UX Engineering, SAP

Lecture (25 min)

AD104 | SAP Build Code: AI development and new service integrations with CAP

Examine new capabilities of SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) in SAP Build Code, and experience how the Joule copilot can speed up your extension development. In addition, find out how service integrations with CAP can reduce development effort, help you avoid pitfalls, and allow for evolution without disruption following the clean core strategy.

David Kunz, Software Architect for CAP @ SAP, SAP

Daniel Schlachter, Software Architect, SAP

Lecture (25 min)

How are you making it easier to build applications?

AD101 | Tailor your UI with SAPUI5 in SAP Build Code

Discover the latest on SAPUI5 development in SAP Build Code, providing a modern user experience across ABAP and SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. Learn how to realize clean core extensibility with new capabilities and how you can future-proof your app with new tools and best practices. Understand why SAP Web Components are strategic and how you can benefit from using them.

Oliver Graeff, Director, Product Management SAP UI5, SAP

Peter Muessig, Chief Architect, SAP

Lecture (25 min)

AD105 | Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build

Learn how to take advantage of interoperability between SAP’s low-code and pro-code solutions. Discover how you can use the graphical modeler in the development environment of SAP Build Code for the ABAP Cloud development model and how your ABAP Cloud projects can be integrated into the lobby of SAP Build solutions.

Anne Keller, Chief Development Expert, SAP

Tim Back, Chief Development Architect, SAP BTP, SAP

Lecture (25 min)

 

SAP Fiori Innovation Day provides more content on UX and AI

If you want a deeper dive into the latest on SAP Fiori and how it enables you to implement AI in your business, please join us for SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Heidelberg on November 19, 2024.

 

Hope to “see” you at SAP TechEd. I might even answer one of your questions during the live chat.

For the Cloud ERP UX Engineering Team, Peter Spielvogel.

entry.title: Attachments2 using HttpClient and C#
entry.content:
Hi. Based on 

  • Postman tool OK
  • .NET B1SLayer OK
  • .NET System.Net.Http.HttpClient Failed

Assuming that the login has been previously performed with the SAPclient object. The Attachments2 method doesn't work with HttpClient in C#. Please see:

var res = "";
var content = new MultipartContent();
var outputFile = new StreamContent(new MemoryStream(File.ReadAllBytes("C:\\Users\\Jorge\\Postman\\files\\helloworld.txt)));

outputFile.Headers.Add("Content-Disposition", $"form-data; name=\"files\"; filename=\"helloworld.txt\"");
outputFile.Headers.Add("Content-Type", "application/octet-stream");
content.Add(outputFile);
var boundary = content.Headers.ContentType!.Parameters.First(o => o.Name.Equals("boundary", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));
boundary.Value = boundary.Value!.Replace("\"", string.Empty);

var response = await SAPclient.PostAsync("https://mycompany.com:50000/b1s/v1/Attachments2", content);
if (response.IsSuccessStatusCode == true && response.StatusCode == HttpStatusCode.Created)
{
Attachments2Response? resA = await response.Content.ReadFromJsonAsync<Attachments2Response>();
res = resA!.AbsoluteEntry.ToString(); 
}

The Response: StatusCode: 400, ReasonPhrase: 'Bad Request', Version: 1.1, Content: System.Net.Http.HttpConnectionResponseContent, Headers:
{
Date: Thu, 12 Sep 2024 20:46:02 GMT
Server: Apache/2.4.54 (Unix)
DataServiceVersion: 3.0
Vary: Accept-Encoding
Connection: close
Transfer-Encoding: chunked
Content-Type: application/json;charset=utf-8
}}

Why and how to fix it?

Thanks.

Jorge

entry.title: Re: Go to> Detail Report from BW query on SAP Portal
entry.content:
Before you think of auth object/permission, you should first check whether there is RRI (Report-Report interface) configured for the source backend Bex/BW query. RRI config is required to give you the option " GoTo>Detail Report " which is nothing but jumping form source query/Report to target query/Report.

Get the backend Bex/BW query technical name of source query and Go to Tcode RSBBS to check if the RRI is configured for that query. If not you need to configure it.

entry.title: Re: sap BTP MDK deployee the changes every time i am gett...
entry.content: Hi @panush_jain

entry.title: Re: cl_attachment_service_api usage in S/4HANA Cloud Publ...
entry.content: Thanks @patrick_winkler! It worked!

entry.title: Re: SAC - Scale percentage in the model
entry.content:
@FrancescoPetrocelli 

When you go the model where this calculated measure is defined, If you scroll down right bottom you should see a section to define formatting /units/scale etc.

 

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: sap BTP MDK deployee the changes every time i am gett...
entry.content: Hi @panush_jain I have the same error, Failed to initialize application data service - Error: [-10340] Download failed due to server error: -857 SERVER_SYNCHRONIZATION_ERROR), where could I find more information about a possible solution, I have already checked the logs in the backend and everything seems to be ok, but I still get the same error.

entry.title: Re: Execute BEx queries at the same time
entry.content:
Not sure if I understand your issue correctly, but you can try this. Chrome has feature called "Background Timer Throttling" that slows down the execution of background JavaScript processes (such as those running BEx queries) to conserve CPU usage.

Steps to Disable Background Timer Throttling:

  1. Open Chrome and type chrome://flags/ in the address bar.
  2. Search for "Throttle Javascript Timers in Background".
  3. Set the feature to "Disabled".
  4. Relaunch Chrome to apply the changes.

This ensures that timers in background tabs aren't throttled, allowing them to keep running as if they were in the foreground.

The disadvantage is that the background tab will keep consuming resources

entry.title: Re: The CDS View has Billed Date in DATS Format in S/4 HA...
entry.content:
Option 1; You can modify the field definition of the Billed Date in your CDS View in S/4HANA as follows:

Use the ABAP.SQL.DATE annotation to ensure the field is represented as a date type. Here is an example:

@ObjectModel.dataType: #DATE
@EndUserText.label: 'Billed Date'
billingDocumentBilledDate as BilledDate

Option 2: In Datasphere, after you have connected and imported the Remote Table, you can create a Graphical View or a SQL View that transforms the STRING field into a DATE field.
Example SQL transformation for converting the STRING to DATE could look like this:

SELECT TO_DATE(BilledDate, 'YYYYMMDD') as BilledDate
FROM Your_Remote_Table;

entry.title: How can I get the user's plant (store)?
entry.content:
Hello SAP's gurus.

I'm developing a Fiori app, and I need to get the plant from the user data.

In the transaction FBP_MAINTAIN_PERS_S with the personalization dialog "DIA_LO_SRS", I can set up the plant (store) for the user.

Is there an odata service that returns the plant (store)?

Best regards,

Victor Carmona.

entry.title: Re: cl_attachment_service_api usage in S/4HANA Cloud Publ...
entry.content:
Dear community,

Following the insightful suggestion from @patrick_winkler to add a COMMIT WORK after the create_attachment() call, all methods are now functioning as expected.

Here's the updated code:

DATA:
  media_resource     TYPE if_attachment_service_api=>ty_s_media_resource,
  attachment_service TYPE REF TO if_attachment_service_api,
  error              TYPE REF TO cx_attachment_service,
  text               TYPE string.

DATA(str) = `{"test": "test01"}`.
DATA(xstr) = cl_abap_conv_codepage=>create_out( codepage = `UTF-8` 
                  )->convert( source = str ).

media_resource = VALUE #( mime_type = `application/json; charset=utf-8`
                          value     = xstr ).

attachment_service = cl_attachment_service_api=>get_instance( ).

TRY.
  attachment_service->create_attachment(
    EXPORTING iv_attachmentobjecttype = 'BUSINESSPARTNER'
              iv_attachmentobjectkey  = '14100014'
              iv_filename             = 'Testing.json'
              iv_documenttype         = 'SAT'
              is_media_resource       = media_resource
    IMPORTING es_attachment           = DATA(attach) ).
    
  COMMIT WORK. " Commit to ensure the attachment is persisted

  CATCH cx_attachment_service INTO error.
    text = error->get_longtext( ).
ENDTRY.

TRY.
  attachment_service->get_list_of_attachments(
    EXPORTING iv_attachmentobjecttype = 'BUSINESSPARTNER'
              iv_attachmentobjectkey  = attach-attachmentobjectkey
              iv_technicalobjecttype  = attach-technicalobjecttype
    IMPORTING et_attachments          = DATA(attachments) ).

  CATCH cx_attachment_service INTO error.
    text = error->get_longtext( ).
ENDTRY.

TRY.
  attachment_service->delete_attachment(
    EXPORTING iv_logicaldocument      = attach-logicaldocument
              iv_archivedocumentid    = attach-archivedocumentid
              iv_technicalobjecttype  = attach-technicalobjecttype
              iv_attachmentobjecttype = 'BUSINESSPARTNER'
              iv_attachmentobjectkey  = attach-attachmentobjectkey
    IMPORTING ev_success              = DATA(success)
              et_messages             = DATA(messages) ).

  CATCH cx_attachment_service INTO error.
    text = error->get_longtext( ).
ENDTRY.


Thanks again for your support!

Best regards,

Dan

entry.title: Re: Hiding Translation and Packages for specific users in...
entry.content:
If you cannot find specific authorizations to hide Translation and Packages, you can create a custom role that limits access to only the features the user needs, excluding the unnecessary ones like Translation and Packages.

Did you check this option: Go to Administration > Workspaces.: Check the Permissions tab for the user and ensure they don’t have access to areas that would expose the Translation or Packages options.

entry.title: Re: Difference between two (dynamic) years in SAC table
entry.content:
@deshmukh 

This is how client calculation currently work, they get attached to the member which you select and create the formula, in your case the years. One Way could be your create restricted measure with the years selected and then create a calculated measure for variance. 

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: How to consume calculated member in Advanced Formulas.
entry.content:
@MoonJun 

How is the performance when reading calculated members with formula in Advance Formula after enabling the CONFIG statement?

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Assertion failed in app F2568 Manage Production Versi...
entry.content: I would suggest you follow the suggestion and file a Case at https://me.sap.com/servicessupport.

entry.title: Moving from Solman to C-ALM, what about users with no external network
entry.content:
Looking forward towards the impending End of Life situation for Solution Manager and the push to C-ALM and FRUN.

My big concern here is how will C-ALM work with customers that do not have or allow external network connectivity.  Since C-ALM will have the Early Watch reporting / recommendations, and we do not have or allow connectivity back to SAP, this is a big concern for us - we use EWR for future planning, weekly reporting, and cyber security compliance.

My question is - how will Earlywatch function in C-ALM to customers that are unable to connect back to SAP?  Is there a plan for some sort of local deployment, integration of EWR functionality into FRUN, or some other method - maybe a longer term Solman deployment for those customers?

entry.title: Re: Moving from Solman to C-ALM, what about users with no external network
entry.content: I don't think that SAP will move away from their Cloud Strategy. So I think you have to find ways how the users can access the Internet. Maybe on special jumphosts.

entry.title: Re: How To hide the "Work Permit" portlet in both Employe...
entry.content:
Hi @PPER1,

Access to employee information can be managed through RBP. Go to the related role and uncheck the Work Permit section. Please ensure that the target group is USA employees.

If this information helped you, please, Accept as Solution.

Cristian Ocampo

entry.title: Re: System functionality for the employees on secondment
entry.content:
Hi @piotr_retecki,

As you mentioned there is no other native functionality besides RBP to manage access to employee information. This also applies to Concurrent Employment and Global Assignment.

If this information helped you, please, Accept as Solution.

Cristian Ocampo

entry.title: Re: Moving from Solman to C-ALM, what about users with no external network
entry.content: our implementation - and many related implementations will 100% not have connectivity back to SAP in any way. Today we get EWR from Solution Manager which is regularly patched. SAP is going to have to provide some mechanism with no connectivity to continue to receive those reports and other solman functions they intend to move to C-ALM.

entry.title: Re: The SAP Reference Architecture for Artificial Intelligence Technology (unofficial)
entry.content:
SAP SIgnavio Plug and Gain infographic Hi Mario,

Yes I understand . 
What is amazing is that even with SAP current innovations in integrating both these products with SAP BTP, SAP is still committed to their old non-sap customers dealing with platforms such as AWS, Azure, Google and IBM. And this was the reason for my initial question to you knowing your experience with such platforms as well.

Immediately after commenting on your blog, I got a notification from SAP Signavio about this Plug and again infographic and as you can see in the infographic link - point 5, the different SAP products involved for a complete lifecycle of digital business innovations initiatives or projects.

Hopefully someone from the SAP Community already did a similar blog to yours or will now share with us SAP’s take.

Thanks again for your leadership in our community,

Regards, Tobi

entry.title: Re: Which Joule functions will be available for Customers...
entry.content:
Hi @cunalir1,

Here you can find Joule Capabilities by module Joule Capabilities | Conversational Patterns.

The next image is just an example. For the latest updates, please check the previous link.

 

If this information helped you, please, Accept as Solution.

Cristian Ocampo

entry.title: Re: SAP extends ERP cloud portfolio with "SAP ERP, private edition, option for Microsoft SQL&qu
entry.content:
Another tool in the toolbox.  Interesting.  

If I understand this correctly (Assuming SQL server is at correct level):

If we have a customer at EHP6 or higher running SQL Server..  We can lift and shift them to RISE and have until 2030 to get to S/4 (this is 2027 + the Extended Support Period).  SAP has already announced ECC customers in RISE will get extended support as part of the normal subscription fee.

If we have a customer at EPH5 or lower running SQL Server.. We can lift and shift them to RISE and have until the end of 2025 to get to S/4 or to something newer than EHP5.

Did I miss anything?  AZURE only I would assume.  
All windows servers or any Linux servers?

entry.title: Re: Typing over SAP delivered Return Reasons
entry.content: We decided to go for it and the updated the values. Surprisingly the updated values display on existing documents, not just new ones.

entry.title: SAP SD
entry.content:
This message sent to me when I do delivery 

No delivery-relevant items in order 0000005528, order type MTOR

Message No. VL455

Diagnosis

The sales order you want to deliver, sales order 0000005528 with order type MTOR, either contains no delivery-relevant items or its delivery-relevant items have already been delivered.

entry.title: Re: How to edit Greetings text on homepage in Sap Success...
entry.content:
Hi @Santhoshsk15,

You can do this using the Manage Languages functionality. All the details can be found here. 3212276 - How to edit or hide the greeting on the latest homepage banner image

If this information helped you, please, Accept as Solution.

Cristian Ocampo

entry.title: Re: GR not possible for delivery xyz: Error code 8
entry.content:
Hi.

Enter transac SM12, find the delivery number and delete the entry.

Regards.

entry.title: Re: ICMR workflow Adjustment and Closing & communication...
entry.content: For your first question, not sure whether note 3171099 Work item tasks getting delayed in My Inbox is relevant.

entry.title: Re: Accumulative sum using ABAP CDS (No AMDP function implementation)
entry.content: @Sandra_RossiIt was caused by the fabulous SAP Community upgrade. A lot of "old" blog posts now have big spaces between paragraphs.

entry.title: Re: Fiori - Selection Expression Field empty in Define Se...
entry.content:
Assume you are implementing GR on S/4 HANA 1909 or onwards, please refer to the following comments from note 2659672 - FAQ About SAP S/4HANA Finance for Group Reporting (On Premise)

To import selections that were created in the Define Selections app in another system on release 1909, use the export and import feature in the Import/Export Validation Settings app.

entry.title: Re: Fiori - Selection Expression Field empty in Define Se...
entry.content:
Please refer to following from note 2659672 - FAQ About SAP S/4HANA Finance for Group Reporting (On Premise)

To import selections that were created in the Define Selections app in another system on release 1909, use the export and import feature in the Import/Export Validation Settings app.
